'''Kenneth Colley''' ([[December 7]], [[1930s#1937|1937]] – [[June 30]], [[2025]]) was an English actor who portrayed [[Admiral]] [[Firmus Piett]] in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']]. Colley is the only actor to play the same [[Galactic Empire|Imperial]] officer in more than one Star Wars film. Other notable roles were that of Jesus in ''Monty Python's the Life of Brian'' and Gerhardt Boldt in ''Hitler: The Last Ten Days'', which also starred fellow Star Wars actors [[Alec Guinness]] ([[Obi-Wan Kenobi]]) and [[Julian Glover]] ([[General]] [[Maximilian Veers]]). Colley also planned to reprise his role of Piett for [[Richard LeParmentier]]'s fan film ''Motti Now''. He died from COVID-19 and pneumonia on 30th June 2025.<ref name="Kenneth Colley obituary">{{WebCite|url=https://www.express.co.uk/celebrity-news/2076974/breaking-kenneth-colley-dead-star-wars|title=Kenneth Colley dead: Star Wars legend dies from Covid complications as tributes flood in|date=2025-07-03|archivedate=20250703132959}}</ref>
